allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking gives branch-solve-merge the structural map that makes branching and merging reflect the real interdependencies instead of arbitrary work splits.
Decomposition gives branch-solve-merge its practical entry point by turning a large problem into logical pieces that can actually be worked.
Simplification strengthens the merge phase by forcing the final answer into a form the audience can actually understand and use.

antagonists.rationale_text: Commitment-bias undermines branch-solve-merge when the team sticks to one merge path even after branch evidence diverges from it.
antagonists.source_quote: continue one merge path despite branch evidence divergence

antagonists.rationale_text: Anchoring weakens branch-solve-merge when the first frame or branch structure hardens too early and later evidence gets forced back into it.
antagonists.source_quote: apply familiar frameworks routinely and unquestioningly

structured_tensions.tension_text: Branch-solve-merge conflicts with commitment-bias when teams continue one merge path despite branch evidence divergence.
structured_tensions.source_quote: branch-solve-merge vs commitment-bias: branch-solve-merge conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when teams continue one merge path despite branch evidence divergence.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on systems and decomposition allies plus one explicit conflict with commitment-bias, so the file preserves that conservative surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation widening beyond the clearly named issue-structuring and anti-premature-merge surface.
open_questions: Feynman-technique is source-real for merge clarity, but the retained set favors already-curated structural allies and the explicit commitment tension.
